name: El Salvador
experiences: San Salvador museums and volcano viewpoints
Santa Ana Volcano and Coatepeque crater-lake views
El Tunco surf sunsets and Pacific beach cafes
Ruta de las Flores coffee towns and waterfalls
Suchitoto colonial streets and Suchitlán boat rides
avoid: May to October is wetter, with afternoon downpours that can muddy the Santa Ana Volcano trail and make rural transfers along Ruta de las Flores slower.
Surf towns such as El Tunco are loudest on Friday and Saturday nights, so light sleepers should book back from the main strip.
Chicken buses are very cheap for short daylight hops, but keep luggage light and avoid tight evening connections between towns.
stopoverNote: Monseñor Óscar Arnulfo Romero International Airport feeds most trips through San Salvador, where Uber works well for urban hops before switching to intercity buses for the west and Suchitoto.

cities.name: San Salvador
cities.notes: Capital base for museums, food, volcano viewpoints, and the easiest place to use Uber before moving onward by bus.

cities.name: Santa Ana
cities.notes: Best base for Tazumal, Santa Ana Volcano, and Lake Coatepeque day trips with a compact historic center.

cities.name: El Tunco
cities.notes: Surf village on the Pacific for sunset beach time, beginner lessons, and easy hops to El Sunzal and nearby waterfalls.

cities.name: Ruta de las Flores
cities.notes: Coffee-town circuit anchored by Juayúa, Apaneca, and Ataco for murals, food fairs, viewpoints, and highland day trips.

cities.name: Suchitoto
cities.notes: Colonial hill town for indigo workshops, lake excursions, and one of the most relaxed cultural stops in the country.

itinerary.sim: Tigo or Claro prepaid/eSIM plans are the simplest option; expect roughly ₹830–₹1,250 for about 8–12GB. Coverage is strong in San Salvador and the west, but download offline maps before volcano, waterfall, and national-park days.

San Salvador.name: San Salvador
San Salvador.note: Stay around San Benito, Escalón, or Santa Elena for safer evenings, easy restaurant access, and quick Uber rides to museums and viewpoints.

days.theme: Historic Core, MARTE & Modern San Benito

activities.name: Iglesia El Rosario and Plaza Libertad walk
Metropolitan Cathedral of San Salvador exterior and civic-center stroll
Museo de Arte de El Salvador (MARTE)
Plaza Futura sunset views and dinner stop
El Boquerón National Park crater viewpoint
Joya de Cerén archaeological site
San Andrés archaeological park
Uber rides within San Salvador for the evening return

Santa Ana.name: Santa Ana
Santa Ana.note: Base yourself near the center if you want to walk to the cathedral and theater, then leave early for both Tazumal and the Santa Ana Volcano trailhead.

activities.name: Santa Ana Cathedral and Parque Libertad walk
Teatro de Santa Ana exterior and lobby visit
Tazumal archaeological park
Casa Blanca archaeological area museum gardens

days.theme: Santa Ana Volcano Hike & Coatepeque Views

activities.name: Santa Ana Volcano hike from Cerro Verde
Cerro Verde viewpoints over Lago de Coatepeque
Lake Coatepeque lakeside lunch stop
Return to Santa Ana by local shuttle or bus

El Tunco.name: El Tunco
El Tunco.note: Choose lodging a lane back from the beach if you want sleep; the compact core is walkable, and boards, lessons, and sunset bars are all close together.

activities.name: Beginner surf lesson at El Tunco or El Sunzal
El Tunco Beach rock formations and sunset walk
Short hop to Playa El Sunzal for watching advanced surfers
Weekend live-music bars on the main strip

Ruta de las Flores.name: Ruta de las Flores
Ruta de las Flores.note: Juayúa or Ataco make the simplest overnight bases; public buses work between towns, but a driver or shuttle saves time if you want waterfalls, coffee, and El Imposible in one stretch.

days.theme: Juayúa Food Trail, Ataco Murals & Coffee Towns

activities.name: Juayúa weekend food festival or central market browsing
Los Chorros de la Calera waterfall outing
Concepción de Ataco murals, plaza, and artisan shops
Café Albania viewpoint stop in Apaneca

days.theme: El Imposible National Park Day Trip

activities.name: El Imposible National Park entry
Guided hike to viewpoints and forest trails
Ahuachapán coffee-country roadside lunch
Evening return to Ataco or Juayúa

meals: Restaurante y Café La Pampa Ahuachapán

Suchitoto.name: Suchitoto
Suchitoto.note: Suchitoto is compact and best enjoyed slowly; most visitors walk the center, then take a tuk-tuk or short transfer to the lake edge or Los Tercios.

days.theme: Colonial Streets, Lake Suchitlán & Indigo Crafts

activities.name: Santa Lucía Church and central plaza walk
Lake Suchitlán boat ride
Centro Arte para la Paz visit
Los Tercios waterfall viewpoint

connections.method: Intercity bus from Terminal de Occidente or frequent coach-style service; Uber is best only for getting to the terminal inside San Salvador.
Intercity bus back via San Salvador or a direct shared shuttle if you want fewer changes.
Chicken bus and local-bus combo via Sonsonate for the cheapest route, or tourist shuttle for a smoother transfer.
Intercity buses with a San Salvador change, or private transfer if you are tight on time.
Regular local bus back to the capital for airport or final-night connections.

extras: El Salvador uses the US dollar locally, but all prices here are converted at roughly ₹83 per dollar for planning consistency.
Uber is reliable inside San Salvador, while westbound and intercity travel is usually easiest by public bus, shuttle, or pre-arranged driver.
Carry small cash for chicken buses, market snacks, trailheads, and national-park add-ons because cards are less useful outside the capital and surf hotels.
